title: End of Evangelion Funimation Petition
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion Funimation Petition             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Mon Dec 14, 2009 10:36 pm Since Manga Entertainment lost the rights to End of Evangelion, I would like Funimation to buy the rights for End of Evangelion. Now that Funimation has the cast for Rebuild of Evangelion, they would use the cast to dub EOE. Therefore, I have created a petition to have Funimation buy the rights. Here is the link: http://www.petitionspot.com/petitions/EndEvangelion Also, it would be appreciated if you give feedback on the petition and my opinion. Thanks for your time.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Dec 14, 2009 10:58 pm I read that as "End of Funimation" petition, as in you wanted Funimation gone and dead.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Mon Dec 14, 2009 11:11 pm Sachi wrote: I read that as "End of Funimation" petition, as in you wanted Funimation gone and dead.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Dec 14, 2009 11:24 pm tomrule123 wrote: That is not what I am saying. Jeez. Didn't you read the description?

[]:             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Mon Dec 14, 2009 11:42 pm ya, i would like it better if they got to work on the rebuild series before anything else.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Dec 14, 2009 11:57 pm Petitions are a complete waste of time. Besides, I actually like the English dub with the sole exception of the added 'squish'.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 15, 2009 12:02 am Petitions are a waste of time. Someone will pick up the license. Given pseudo-ADV being in an extremely weird state of flux right now, I doubt they would be able to scrape together the money for the license. Also, to be noted is that whoever picks up the license will have to re-dub it anyway. So if it's not done by Funimation, expect yet another permutation of the English cast.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Tue Dec 15, 2009 12:07 am I'd just like to see a better transfer. I couldn't care less about the dub. I didn't mind the dub done on EoE, but at this point there are so many different VAs involved that it'll never be repeated (and the best English VAs on the show aren't even in the industry anymore to my knowledge).

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Tue Dec 15, 2009 12:13 am I do have this nagging feeling that it will be good while before we see a rerelease because Gainax will try to hold out for the highest price they think they can get. If I remember right, I thought I read on a thread a long time ago that Manga only got the licence beause ADV wasn't willing to the price Gainax wanted. Am I mistaken?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 15, 2009 12:28 am Merridian wrote: I'd just like to see a better transfer. I couldn't care less about the dub.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Dec 15, 2009 2:43 am If funimation does decide to pick it up, I hope they don’t go their usual route and do a bunch of dub rewrites for the English script like they usually like to do for a lot of their releases.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Tue Dec 15, 2009 2:59 am NemZ wrote: Petitions are a complete waste of time. Besides, I actually like the English dub with the sole exception of the added 'squish'.

[]:             Quote Post by LordCaldwell » Tue Dec 15, 2009 8:42 am I watched EoE again last night. I have concluded that the manga ldt or whoever that made the movie fails at life. The squish was retarted, too much of the diolauge was stupid or repeditive, and makato definatly ISNT BLACK! I mean seriously, I love that movie and this is my favorite anime of al time, but WHAT THE FUCK WAS MANGA THINKING??? they even changed the color to some kind of pastel, and it seemed like some of the characters were drawn slightly differently. I hope ADV films takes the movie rights.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Tue Dec 15, 2009 9:12 am LordCaldwell wrote: I mean seriously, I love that movie and this is my favorite anime of al time, but WHAT THE FUCK WAS MANGA THINKING??? they even changed the color to some kind of pastel, and it seemed like some of the characters were drawn slightly differently. I hope ADV films takes the movie rights.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Tue Dec 15, 2009 9:57 am Iron Rooster wrote: NemZ wrote: Petitions are a complete waste of time. Besides, I actually like the English dub with the sole exception of the added 'squish'.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Dec 15, 2009 10:34 am A real live handwritten letter is going to be more effective than any on-line thing. ANN gives Mailing address: FUNimation Entertainment, Ltd. 6851 NE Loop 820 Fort Worth, TX 76180-9012

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 15, 2009 11:07 am Tines speaks the truth. Nobody gives a shit about online petitions. No one. They're so easy to create, administer, and sign, that it doesn't show any real level of devotion. If you wrote a concise, well worded letter, and mailed it to them, they would be far more receptive. Hell, even a well worded email would be a far better use of your time.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Tue Dec 15, 2009 12:01 pm What? No! I would hate to see EoE redubbed. I mean EoE is the final 2 episodes of NGE. It would be wierd if you watched a show all the way then right before the end they change all the actors... Not to mention its a great dub anyways. Though i would love Funi to license EoE and the real D&R for blu ray... with the squishes removed *shudder* What was that!? ...although if funi licensed NGE, EOE, D&R and RoE and redubbed them all i wouldn't complain, i still have the originals and i would buy them again, gladly :/

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 15, 2009 12:22 pm skikes wrote: It would be wierd if you watched a show all the way then right before the end they change all the actors...

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Tue Dec 15, 2009 12:53 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: skikes wrote: It would be wierd if you watched a show all the way then right before the end they change all the actors...
